Center: China Nuclear Data Center, Beijing, China
ref: Jour: Chinese Physics C, Vol.48, p.054104 (2024)
title: Cross sections for 14 MeV neutron interaction with lutetium isotopes and their theoretical excitation functions

x4freetext: Cross sections for 14 MeV neutron interaction with
lutetium isotopes and their theoretical excitation

x4codes.hlp: Pingdingshan Univ., Pingdingshan, China, People`s Republic

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Chinese Physics C, Vol.48, p.054104 (2024)

x4codes.hlp: Southwest Inst.Nucl.Phys.and Chem.,Mianyang,Sichuan, China, People`s Republic

x4freetext: The yield of the quasi-monoenergetic neutrons
from the T(d,n)4He reaction was approximately
5E10 n/s. The deuteron beam energy was 255 keV, beam
current was 250-320 uA, and thickness of the T-Ti
target in the neutron generator used was
Disc-shaped natural pure lutetium plates with 99.9%
The neutron flux was monitored by measuring
the accompanying alpha-particles with an Au-Si
surface barrier detector to correct small variations
A HPGe detector (ORTEC GEM-60P, made in USA
and featuring 70.1 mm crystal diameter and 72.3 mm
crystal length), with a relative efficiency of 68%
and an energy resolution of 1.69 keV at 1332 keV

x4codes.shortRef: Book: Physik Daten/Physics Data,Series,Karlsruhe, Vol.13-5, p.183 (1990)

x4freetext: The total correction include self-absorption of the
gamma-ray, coincidence summing of the cascade
gamma-rays of the studied nuclide, and measurement
geometry of lutetium samples and niobium plates,
